# t2.1.1 - Deterministic Clean-Build Smoke Resolution

## Summary

The clean-build smoke blocker is an active runtime lock, not a broken Maven build. `liberty:run`
starts Open Liberty from `target\liberty\wlp`, and the embedded Derby datasource in
`src/main/liberty/config/server.xml` points at `${shared.resource.dir}/data/tradedb`, which resolves
under that same `target` tree. While the server is live, Derby keeps
`target\liberty\wlp\usr\shared\resources\data\tradedb\seg0\cd1.dat` open, so `mvn clean` cannot
delete `target`.

## Resolution

Use an explicit stop-before-clean smoke flow. The repository now includes
`scripts/smoke-clean-build.ps1`, which:

- runs `mvn -B liberty:stop` to release the project-local Liberty/Derby file lock
- runs `mvn -B -DskipTests clean package` immediately after lock release

This keeps the resolution narrow and deterministic for the current Liberty baseline without
changing packaging, Derby seed data, or runtime topology during analysis.

## Evidence

- Reproduced failure: `mvn -B -DskipTests clean package`
- Failure mode: `maven-clean-plugin` could not delete
  `target\liberty\wlp\usr\shared\resources\data\tradedb\seg0\cd1.dat`
- Active locking process: Liberty runtime Java process launched from
  `target\liberty\wlp\bin\tools\ws-server.jar --batch-file run defaultServer`
- Discriminating check: `mvn -B liberty:stop` succeeded
- Post-stop verification: `mvn -B -DskipTests clean package` succeeded

## Operational Guidance

- Treat `target\liberty\wlp` as mutable runtime state while Liberty is running.
- For deterministic baseline smoke evidence, stop Liberty before any `clean` goal.
- If future tasks need concurrent runtime and build activity, move mutable Derby runtime data out of
  `target`; that is a later design choice, not required to unblock analysis evidence now.

## Test Results

- Command: `pwsh -File scripts/smoke-clean-build.ps1`
- Passed: 1
- Failed: 0
- Skipped: 0
